section.name: nginx-multi-domain
section.summary: Multi-domain nginx server block pattern, shared snippets, Cloudflare origin certificate setup, and enable/test/reload workflow.

rule.statement: Use shared snippets (/etc/nginx/snippets/) for proxy-params, security headers, and SSL params; never copy-paste these blocks into each site config.
rule.rationale: Copied blocks diverge over time. When a security header needs updating across 5 sites, a single snippets/security-headers.conf edit covers all of them in one nginx reload.

rule.statement: Always run nginx -t before reload; a config syntax error stops the reload and leaves nginx serving stale config without error feedback.
rule.rationale: nginx reload only takes effect if the new config parses successfully. Without testing first, a typo in a new site block causes a silent failure where the intended site config never loads.

rule.statement: Use Cloudflare Origin Certificates with Full (Strict) SSL mode; origin certs are free, have 15-year validity, and require no renewal automation.
rule.rationale: Let's Encrypt certs require renewal every 90 days and a certbot cron job. With Cloudflare acting as the termination point, origin certs only need to be trusted by Cloudflare — no public CA required.

example.code: # Install a Cloudflare origin certificate for a domain sudo mkdir -p /etc/ssl/example.com # Paste cert from Cloudflare dashboard → SSL/TLS → Origin Server → Create Certificate sudo nano /etc/ssl/example.com/cert.pem sudo nano /etc/ssl/example.com/key.pem sudo chmod 600 /etc/ssl/example.com/key.pem sudo chmod 644 /etc/ssl/example.com/cert.pem

# Standard workflow to enable a new site sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/example.com /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
# Verify site is active curl -I https://example.com

antipattern.description: Editing site configs directly in sites-enabled/ instead of sites-available/.
antipattern.reason: sites-enabled/ should contain only symlinks. Editing files directly in sites-enabled/ breaks the sites-available/ as the authoritative config location and complicates backup and version control.

section.reference: Generic nginx server block template with HTTP redirect, SSL, proxy, WebSocket
